name: Pyrvinium
description: Pyrvinium is an anthelmintic effective for pinworms. Several forms of pyrvinium have been prepared with variable counter anions, such as halides, tosylate, triflate and pamoate. Pyrvinum's anti-cancer properties are currently under investigation.
indication: Pyrvinium was once used in the treatment of pinworm infestations [T39].
mechanismOfAction: Pyrvinium is believed to interfere with glucose uptake by pinworms [T39]. Pyrvinium is also thought to inhibit mitochondrial respiration complex 1 and suppress the unfolded protein response [A19733]. It is also believed to supress the Wnt pathway by activating casein kinase 1α. These properties have led to the investigation of pyrvinium's activity against cancers like intestinal polyposis [A19734].
pharmacodynamics: Pyrvinium is an anthelmintic agent which acts to kill pinworms [T39].

interactions.name: Chloroquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Pyrvinium can be decreased when it is combined with Chloroquine.

interactions.name: Amodiaquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Pyrvinium can be decreased when it is combined with Amodiaquine.

interactions.name: Primaquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Pyrvinium can be decreased when it is combined with Primaquine.

interactions.name: Hydroxychloroquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Pyrvinium can be decreased when it is combined with Hydroxychloroquine.
